subject: Commonly Used Discrete Random Variables
question: Show that the Geometric distribution with parameter $p$ satisfies the equation $\Pr(X > i+j \mid X > i) = \Pr(X > j)$. This says that the Geometric distribution satisfies the memoryless property
answer: We have $\Pr(X > i+j \mid X > i) = \frac{\Pr(X > i+j, X > i)}{\Pr(X > i)} = \frac{\Pr(X > i+j)}{\Pr(X > i)} = \frac{(1-p)^{i+j}}{(1-p)^i} = (1-p)^j = \Pr(X > j)$
explanation: Answer: We have $\Pr(X > i+j \mid X > i) = \frac{\Pr(X > i+j, X > i)}{\Pr(X > i)} = \frac{\Pr(X > i+j)}{\Pr(X > i)} = \frac{(1-p)^{i+j}}{(1-p)^i} = (1-p)^j = \Pr(X > j)$
